name: two-changes-to-the-video-20260408-0346
idea:
Two changes to the video editor's shot grouping behavior.

**Change 1: Restrict per-clip right-click menu when clip is inside a pinned shot group**

Background: A "pinned shot group" visually groups clips on the timeline under a shot label. The shot label has its own context menu (ShotGroupContextMenu) for shot-level operations (Deconstruct, Delete, Switch to Final Video, Generate Video, etc.). The per-clip right-click menu (ClipContextMenu in src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx) currently shows shot-management actions (Create Shot, list of shots, Generate Video, Jump to Shot) AND timeline-geometry actions (Split Here, Delete Clip) AND asset-state actions (Update to current variant, Dismiss reminder) regardless of whether the clip is in a pinned shot group.

Principle: A pinned shot group is the unit of timeline editing. Clips inside it are no longer first-class timeline citizens; they're 'images inside this shot.' So per-clip operations affecting timeline geometry or shot membership don't belong on the clip's right-click menu when it's in a shot group.

Required behavior when isInPinnedShotGroup === true:
- Show ONLY asset/variant state actions: 'Update to current variant' and 'Dismiss reminder' (when applicable).
- HIDE: Split Here, Delete Clip, Delete N clips, Mute/unmute, Create Shot, list of existing shots with Jump/Generate buttons, Generate Video.
- If neither asset-state action applies, the right-click should be a no-op (don't open an empty menu).

When isInPinnedShotGroup === false -> current behavior unchanged.

Implementation notes:
- shotGroupClipIds: Set<string> already exists in TimelineEditor.tsx (around line 321).
- getActionRender in TimelineEditor.tsx (~line 542) renders each <ClipAction>. Pass new prop isInPinnedShotGroup={shotGroupClipIds.has(action.id)}.
- Add isInPinnedShotGroup?: boolean to ClipAction props and thread it into ClipContextMenuProps.
- In ClipContextMenu render (ClipAction.tsx:74-206), gate the hidden sections on !isInPinnedShotGroup.
- In ClipActionComponent.handleContextMenu, if isInPinnedShotGroup AND no asset-state actions, early-return without opening menu.
- Tests in ClipAction.test.tsx may need updates.

**Change 2: Auto-shift adjacent clips inside a shot group to keep the group contiguous when one is resized**

Background: A pinned shot group requires constituent clips to be contiguous. Validation in TimelineCanvas.tsx positionedShotGroups (around line 425-477) drops a group if any sibling pair has a gap. When user resizes a middle clip's edge inward, it creates a gap and group disappears.

Required behavior: When user resizes an edge of a clip inside a pinned shot group:
- If drag LEFT edge of middle/last clip -> previous clip's RIGHT edge follows, eating the gap.
- If drag RIGHT edge of middle/first clip -> next clip's LEFT edge follows.
- For FIRST clip in group, its LEFT edge is free. For LAST clip, its RIGHT edge is free.
- If clip NOT in pinned shot group, behavior unchanged.

Implementation notes:
- Resize state in TimelineCanvas.tsx: handleResizePointerDown, updateResize, endResize, resizeSessionRef, resizeOverrides, ~lines 549-720.
- During updateResize, compute override for adjacent clip in shot group.
- Enforce min-duration on adjacent clip.
- Call setResizeOverrides for BOTH clips.
- On endResize, both overrides flushed via onActionResizeEnd. May need to extend onActionResizeEnd to take array, or call twice.
- Find shot group via shotGroups prop.
- Skip snap-to-sibling-within-group (auto-shift replaces it).

Files likely to change:
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.test.tsx
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx

Constraints:
- Scoped to video editor tool only.
- No DB/migration/edge function changes.
- Follow CLAUDE.md rules.
- Do not regress existing tests (one pre-existing failure about data-shot-group-kind='pinned' is unrelated, leave it).
